bull.b: Nine consecutive quarters of year-over-year revenue growth, including Q1 2026 +10% to $22M, indicate durable demand and product-market fit across channels. Persistent top-line expansion supports scale economics and increases the likelihood management can drive margins and operational leverage over time.

A materially stronger cash balance (about $32M vs $10M year-end 2025) provides multi-quarter runway to execute launches, scale manufacturing, and finish digital/AI rollouts without immediate dilution. This liquidity supports the plan to reach cash-flow positivity in 2026 if execution proceeds as guided.

Meaningful commercial agreements and licensing positions (large developer pipelines and a multi-year deal with GE/Dolby) create structural distribution and monetization channels. These partnerships can accelerate deployments, enable geographic scale, and create recurring/licensing revenue streams if standards and adopt

bear.b: Negative operating and free cash flow on a trailing-twelve-month basis shows the business still consumes capital to grow. Until sustained positive operating cash flow is achieved, SKYX will remain dependent on external financing, which can dilute shareholders or constrain investments if market conditions tighten.

Key licensing and standards-driven revenue depends on industry and code adoption, which management notes is a lengthy process. Slow regulatory standardization creates multi-year timing uncertainty for mandated demand and limits the immediacy of licensing as a reliable growth driver.

Significant revenue upside depends on executing large retail rollouts, completing AI e-commerce upgrades, and scaling manufacturing. Failure or delays in expanding big-box presence and finishing digital initiatives would slow margin improvement and prolong losses despite product demand signals.
